finding.recommendation: Promote only the generic backend adapters into deep-loop-runtime: a parameterized runtime-capabilities resolver and the loop-lock CLI adapter. Keep per-mode capability manifests in deep-loop-workflows. Do not promote verify-yaml-script-paths.sh to runtime; move it to deep-loop-workflows as workflow validation. Move resolveArtifactRoot into deep-loop-runtime as shared artifact topology, but move emitResourceMap into deep-loop-workflows shared synthesis code rather than runtime, eliminating system-spec-kit cross-imports without polluting the backend with mode deliverable rendering.
key_tradeoffs: Centralizing the resolver removes duplicate code, but the capability manifests must stay mode-scoped to preserve WebFetch/tool-surface differences.
Moving loop-lock.cjs to runtime aligns the CLI with its existing runtime implementation, but requires command YAML and documentation repoints.
Keeping the YAML verifier out of runtime preserves the backend boundary, but makes deep-loop-workflows responsible for expanding verifier coverage across all migrated commands.

Moving resolveArtifactRoot out of system-spec-kit makes the two-skill architecture self-contained, while keeping emitResourceMap out of runtime preserves the distinction between backend topology and workflow deliverable rendering.
risks: Historical generated configs may still reference old resolver paths unless migrated or explicitly treated as archival.
A runtime capability resolver that defaults to one manifest would flatten mode permissions and violate byte-identical behavior.
Council's capability manifest schema differs from research/review, so all-mode resolver support needs explicit schema handling if required.
Changing YAML resolver invocation from node -e to a script must preserve the exact JSON output shape.
Verifier expansion beyond research/review depends on the final merged command and mode layout.
depends_on: Q-CMD command surface stability and YAML migration scope
Q-AGENT retained versus consolidated native agent names
Q-ADVISOR merged skill metadata and alias strategy
open_questions: Should the migrated capability resolver support council's object-shaped runtime_capabilities.json or remain scoped to the research/review array schema?
Will existing research/review/context packets be migrated in place, or is byte-identical acceptance limited to new single-executor runs?
Should artifact-root be exposed only as a library import or also as an official runtime script with JSON stdout?
Should the YAML verifier expand to all eight deep commands in the same migration phase or only after each mode is repointed?
